pwrMsc.school: Wrocław University of Science and Technology

highlights: Developed and maintained web services in the Volvo IT environment.
Worked with the .NET stack (C#, ASP) and data integration (SSIS).
Used MS SQL Server and VB scripts in day-to-day tasks.
outcomes: Helped keep internal web services running smoothly.
Learned delivery practices in a large, international IT organization.
Gained first production experience with .NET and SQL Server.

highlights: Developed and maintained web services during a student internship.
Worked with .NET (C#, ASP) and MS SQL Server.
outcomes: Strengthened software engineering basics on a real project.
Learned the maintenance cycle of web applications in an enterprise organization.

highlights: Designed and developed backend systems for an EV charging infrastructure operator, supporting CPO integrations via OCPI, OCPP, and BOCPP protocols.
Rebuilt the API layer (GraphQL Federation, gRPC, REST), enabling flexible data access and faster product feature development.
Supported migration of responsibilities and data to a single source of truth model, reducing inconsistency risk across the system.
outcomes: Simplified data access for product teams through a consistent API layer.
Shortened time to deliver new integrations thanks to a flexible API architecture.
Laid the foundation for further growth of a platform serving the EV ecosystem.

highlights: Building our own products and services.
Gaining experience through practice and experimentation.
outcomes: Built and launched the AR Flashbulb platform supporting the sales process.
Designed and launched Elepantry — a platform integrating multiple cloud service providers in one place.
Gained experience building products from concept through development to commercialization.

highlights: Developed a payments platform handling UK BACS and Faster Payments systems.
Designed solutions that improved transaction processing reliability and failure resilience.
Designed failure-recovery mechanisms that increased payment processing reliability.
outcomes: Helped maintain continuity of payment processing critical to Form3 customers.
Reduced the impact of failures through better recovery handling and system monitoring.
Made safer evolution of the payments platform possible through greater observability and stability.

highlights: Designed and built from scratch a central gaming platform that became the foundation for multiple games.
Introduced a video streaming system for hosts before gameplay started (HLS).
Developed abuse detection and prevention mechanisms (anti-cheating).
outcomes: Delivered a scalable platform enabling fast creation and launch of subsequent games.
Optimized infrastructure usage, supporting efficient platform growth.
Accelerated development of new features and product experiments through shared platform components.

highlights: Delivered software solutions for clients using JVM, Go, and cloud technologies.
Designed and developed distributed systems based on microservices, asynchronous communication, and the cloud (AWS, Azure).
Automated software delivery through CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and automation tooling.
outcomes: Designed and deployed one of the first serverless systems for monitoring elevator operations and detecting situations requiring intervention.
Built a package-flow management system in a logistics environment, including notification handling mechanisms.

highlights: Led the RDS team (14 people: Wrocław, London, Bangalore).
Managed the backlog, business requirements, and communication with management.
Owned technical design, code reviews, and team skill development.
Maintained continuity of a critical ETL process supporting VaR calculation and bank risk-related decisions.
outcomes: Gained experience working in a global financial organization and with the requirements placed on risk management systems.

highlights: Developed a Learning Management System (J2EE/Spring) — from requirements and ticket analysis through to production releases.

highlights: Owned subscriber migration between operator network clusters.
Designed and built FatMT — an ETL tool automating subscriber data migration.

outcomes: Cut migration delivery time from about 2500 to about 200 hours.
